NKJV.1: Now after the Sabbath, as the first day of the week began to dawn, Mary Magdalene and the other Mary came to see the tomb.
NKJV.2: And behold, there was a great earthquake; for an angel of the Lord descended from heaven, and came and rolled back the stone from the door, and sat on it.
NKJV.3: His countenance was like lightning, and his clothing as white as snow.
NKJV.4: And the guards shook for fear of him, and became like dead men.
NKJV.5: But the angel answered and said to the women, “Do not be afraid, for I know that you seek Jesus who was crucified.
NKJV.6: He is not here; for He is risen, as He said. Come, see the place where the Lord lay.
NKJV.7: And go quickly and tell His disciples that He is risen from the dead, and indeed He is going before you into Galilee; there you will see Him. Behold, I have told you.”
NKJV.8: So they went out quickly from the tomb with fear and great joy, and ran to bring His disciples word.
NKJV.9: And as they went to tell His disciples, behold, Jesus met them, saying, “Rejoice!” So they came and held Him by the feet and worshiped Him.
NKJV.10: Then Jesus said to them, “Do not be afraid. Go and tell My brethren to go to Galilee, and there they will see Me.”
NKJV.11: Now while they were going, behold, some of the guard came into the city and reported to the chief priests all the things that had happened.
NKJV.12: When they had assembled with the elders and consulted together, they gave a large sum of money to the soldiers,
NKJV.13: saying, “Tell them, ‘His disciples came at night and stole Him away while we slept.’
NKJV.14: And if this comes to the governor’s ears, we will appease him and make you secure.”
NKJV.15: So they took the money and did as they were instructed; and this saying is commonly reported among the Jews until this day.
NKJV.16: Then the eleven disciples went away into Galilee, to the mountain which Jesus had appointed for them.
NKJV.17: When they saw Him, they worshiped Him; but some doubted.
NKJV.18: And Jesus came and spoke to them, saying, “All authority has been given to Me in heaven and on earth.
NKJV.19: Go therefore and  make disciples of all the nations, baptizing them in the name of the Father and of the Son and of the Holy Spirit,
NKJV.20: teaching them to observe all things that I have commanded you; and lo, I am with you always, even to the end of the age.” Amen.

ESV.1: Now after the Sabbath, toward the dawn of the first day of the week, Mary Magdalene and the other Mary went to see the tomb.
ESV.2: And behold, there was a great earthquake, for an angel of the Lord descended from heaven and came and rolled back the stone and sat on it.
ESV.3: His appearance was like lightning, and his clothing white as snow.
ESV.4: And for fear of him the guards trembled and became like dead men.
ESV.5: But the angel said to the women, “Do not be afraid, for I know that you seek Jesus who was crucified.
ESV.6: He is not here, for he has risen, as he said. Come, see the place where he  lay.
ESV.7: Then go quickly and tell his disciples that he has risen from the dead, and behold, he is going before you to Galilee; there you will see him. See, I have told you.”
ESV.8: So they departed quickly from the tomb with fear and great joy, and ran to tell his disciples.
ESV.9: And behold, Jesus met them and said, “Greetings!” And they came up and took hold of his feet and worshiped him.
ESV.10: Then Jesus said to them, “Do not be afraid; go and tell my brothers to go to Galilee, and there they will see me.”
ESV.11: While they were going, behold, some of the guard went into the city and told the chief priests all that had taken place.
ESV.12: And when they had assembled with the elders and taken counsel, they gave a sufficient sum of money to the soldiers
ESV.13: and said, “Tell people, ‘His disciples came by night and stole him away while we were asleep.’
ESV.14: And if this comes to the governor's ears, we will satisfy him and keep you out of trouble.”
ESV.15: So they took the money and did as they were directed. And this story has been spread among the Jews to this day.
ESV.16: Now the eleven disciples went to Galilee, to the mountain to which Jesus had directed them.
ESV.17: And when they saw him they worshiped him, but some doubted.
ESV.18: And Jesus came and said to them,  “All authority in heaven and on earth has been given to me.
ESV.19: Go therefore and make disciples of all nations, baptizing them in  the name of the Father and of the Son and of the Holy Spirit,
ESV.20: teaching them to observe all that I have commanded you. And behold, I am with you always, to the end of the age.”

NASB.1: Now after the Sabbath, as it began to dawn toward the first [day] of the week, Mary Magdalene and the other Mary came to look at the grave.
NASB.2: And behold, a severe earthquake had occurred, for an angel of the Lord descended from heaven and came and rolled away the stone and sat upon it.
NASB.3: And his appearance was like lightning, and his clothing as white as snow.
NASB.4: The guards shook for fear of him and became like dead men.
NASB.5: The angel said to the women, 'Do not be afraid; for I know that you are looking for Jesus who has been crucified.
NASB.6: 'He is not here, for He has risen, just as He said. Come, see the place where He was lying.
NASB.7: 'Go quickly and tell His disciples that He has risen from the dead; and behold, He is going ahead of you into Galilee, there you will see Him; behold, I have told you.'
NASB.8: And they left the tomb quickly with fear and great joy and ran to report it to His disciples.
NASB.9: And behold, Jesus met them and greeted them. And they came up and took hold of His feet and worshiped Him.
NASB.10: Then Jesus said to them, 'Do not be afraid; go and take word to My brethren to leave for Galilee, and there they will see Me.'
NASB.11: Now while they were on their way, some of the guard came into the city and reported to the chief priests all that had happened.
NASB.12: And when they had assembled with the elders and consulted together, they gave a large sum of money to the soldiers,
NASB.13: and said, 'You are to say, 'His disciples came by night and stole Him away while we were asleep.'
NASB.14: 'And if this should come to the governor's ears, we will win him over and keep you out of trouble.'
NASB.15: And they took the money and did as they had been instructed; and this story was widely spread among the Jews, [and is] to this day.
NASB.16: But the eleven disciples proceeded to Galilee, to the mountain which Jesus had designated.
NASB.17: When they saw Him, they worshiped [Him]; but some were doubtful.
NASB.18: And Jesus came up and spoke to them, saying, 'All authority has been given to Me in heaven and on earth.
NASB.19: 'Go therefore and make disciples of all the nations, baptizing them in the name of the Father and the Son and the Holy Spirit,
NASB.20: teaching them to observe all that I commanded you; and lo, I am with you always, even to the end of the age.'

NLT.1: Early on Sunday morning, as the new day was dawning, Mary Magdalene and the other Mary went out to visit the tomb.
NLT.2: Suddenly there was a great earthquake! For an angel of the Lord came down from heaven, rolled aside the stone, and sat on it.
NLT.3: His face shone like lightning, and his clothing was as white as snow.
NLT.4: The guards shook with fear when they saw him, and they fell into a dead faint.
NLT.5: Then the angel spoke to the women. “Don’t be afraid!” he said. “I know you are looking for Jesus, who was crucified.
NLT.6: He isn’t here! He is risen from the dead, just as he said would happen. Come, see where his body was lying.
NLT.7: And now, go quickly and tell his disciples that he has risen from the dead, and he is going ahead of you to Galilee. You will see him there. Remember what I have told you.”
NLT.8: The women ran quickly from the tomb. They were very frightened but also filled with great joy, and they rushed to give the disciples the angel’s message.
NLT.9: And as they went, Jesus met them and greeted them. And they ran to him, grasped his feet, and worshiped him.
NLT.10: Then Jesus said to them, “Don’t be afraid! Go tell my brothers to leave for Galilee, and they will see me there.”
NLT.11: As the women were on their way, some of the guards went into the city and told the leading priests what had happened.
NLT.12: A meeting with the elders was called, and they decided to give the soldiers a large bribe.
NLT.13: They told the soldiers, “You must say, ‘Jesus’ disciples came during the night while we were sleeping, and they stole his body.’
NLT.14: If the governor hears about it, we’ll stand up for you so you won’t get in trouble.”
NLT.15: So the guards accepted the bribe and said what they were told to say. Their story spread widely among the Jews, and they still tell it today.
NLT.16: Then the eleven disciples left for Galilee, going to the mountain where Jesus had told them to go.
NLT.17: When they saw him, they worshiped him — but some of them doubted!
NLT.18: Jesus came and told his disciples, “I have been given all authority in heaven and on earth.
NLT.19: Therefore, go and make disciples of all the nations, baptizing them in the name of the Father and the Son and the Holy Spirit.
NLT.20: Teach these new disciples to obey all the commands I have given you. And be sure of this: I am with you always, even to the end of the age.”

WEB.1: Now after the Sabbath, as it began to dawn on the first day of the week, Mary Magdalene and the other Mary came to see the tomb.
WEB.2: Behold, there was a great earthquake, for an angel of the Lord descended from the sky, and came and rolled away the stone from the door, and sat on it.
WEB.3: His appearance was like lightning, and his clothing white as snow.
WEB.4: For fear of him, the guards shook, and became like dead men.
WEB.5: The angel answered the women, “Don’t be afraid, for I know that you seek Jesus, who has been crucified.
WEB.6: He is not here, for he has risen, just like he said. Come, see the place where the Lord was lying.
WEB.7: Go quickly and tell his disciples, ‘He has risen from the dead, and behold, he goes before you into Galilee; there you will see him.’ Behold, I have told you.”
WEB.8: They departed quickly from the tomb with fear and great joy, and ran to bring his disciples word.
WEB.9: As they went to tell his disciples, behold, Jesus met them, saying, “Rejoice!” They came and took hold of his feet, and worshiped him.
WEB.10: Then Jesus said to them, “Don’t be afraid. Go tell my brothers   that they should go into Galilee, and there they will see me.”
WEB.11: Now while they were going, behold, some of the guards came into the city, and told the chief priests all the things that had happened.
WEB.12: When they were assembled with the elders, and had taken counsel, they gave a large amount of silver to the soldiers,
WEB.13: saying, “Say that his disciples came by night, and stole him away while we slept.
WEB.14: If this comes to the governor’s ears, we will persuade him and make you free of worry.”
WEB.15: So they took the money and did as they were told. This saying was spread abroad among the Jews, and continues until today.
WEB.16: But the eleven disciples went into Galilee, to the mountain where Jesus had sent them.
WEB.17: When they saw him, they bowed down to him, but some doubted.
WEB.18: Jesus came to them and spoke to them, saying, “All authority has been given to me in heaven and on earth.
WEB.19: Go,  and make disciples of all nations, baptizing them in the name of the Father and of the Son and of the Holy Spirit,
WEB.20: teaching them to observe all things that I commanded you. Behold, I am with you always, even to the end of the age.” Amen.

CSB17.1: After the Sabbath, <sup>ⓕ</sup> as the first day of the week was dawning, Mary Magdalene and the other Mary went to view the tomb.
CSB17.2: There was a violent earthquake, because an angel of the Lord descended from heaven and approached the tomb. He rolled back the stone and was sitting on it. <sup>ⓖ</sup>
CSB17.3: His appearance was like lightning, and his clothing was as white as snow. <sup>ⓗ</sup>
CSB17.4: The guards were so shaken by fear of him that they became like dead men.
CSB17.5: The angel told the women, “Don’t be afraid, because I know you are looking for Jesus who was crucified. <sup>ⓘ</sup>
CSB17.6: He is not here. For he has risen, just as he said. Come and see the place where he lay. <sup>ⓙ</sup>
CSB17.7: Then go quickly and tell his disciples, <sup>ⓚ</sup> ‘He has risen from the dead and indeed he is going ahead of you to Galilee; you will see him there.’ Listen, I have told you.” <sup>ⓛ</sup>
CSB17.8: So, departing quickly from the tomb with fear and great joy, they ran to tell his disciples the news.
CSB17.9: Just then <sup>[1]</sup> Jesus met them and said, “Greetings!” They came up, took hold of his feet, and worshiped him. <sup>ⓜ</sup>
CSB17.10: Then Jesus told them, “Do not be afraid. Go and tell my brothers to leave for Galilee, and they will see me there.” <sup>ⓝ</sup>
CSB17.11: As they were on their way, some of the guards came into the city and reported to the chief priests everything that had happened.
CSB17.12: After the priests <sup>[2]</sup> had assembled with the elders and agreed on a plan, they gave the soldiers a large sum of money
CSB17.13: and told them, “Say this, ‘His disciples came during the night and stole him while we were sleeping.’
CSB17.14: If this reaches the governor’s ears, we will deal with <sup>[3]</sup> him and keep you out of trouble.” <sup>ⓐ</sup>
CSB17.15: They took the money and did as they were instructed, and this story has been spread among Jewish people to this day. <sup>ⓑ</sup>
CSB17.16: The eleven disciples traveled to Galilee, to the mountain where Jesus had directed them. <sup>ⓒ</sup>
CSB17.17: When they saw him, they worshiped, <sup>[4]</sup> but some doubted.
CSB17.18: Jesus came near and said to them, “All authority has been given to me in heaven and on earth. <sup>ⓓ</sup>
CSB17.19: Go, therefore, and make disciples <sup>ⓔ</sup> of <sup>[5]</sup>all nations, <sup>ⓕ</sup> baptizing <sup>ⓖ</sup> them in the name of the Father and of the Son and of the Holy Spirit,
CSB17.20: teaching them to observe everything I have commanded you. And remember, <sup>[6]</sup>I am with you always, <sup>[7]</sup> to the end of the age.” <sup>ⓗ</sup>

AMP.1: Now after the Sabbath, near dawn of the first day of the week, Mary Magdalene and the other Mary went to look at the tomb.
AMP.2: And a great earthquake had occurred, for an angel of the Lord descended from heaven and came and rolled away the stone [from the opening of the tomb], and sat on it.
AMP.3: The angel's appearance was like lightning, and his clothes were as white as snow.
AMP.4: The guards shook, paralyzed with fear [at the sight] of him and became like dead men [pale and immobile].
AMP.5: But the angel said to the women, "Do not be afraid; for I know that you are looking for Jesus who has been crucified.
AMP.6: He is not here, for He has risen, just as He said [He would]. Come! See the place where He was lying.
AMP.7: Then go quickly and tell His disciples that He has risen from the dead; and behold, He is going ahead of you into Galilee [as He promised]. There you will see Him; behold, I have told you."
AMP.8: So the women left the tomb quickly with fear and great joy, and ran to tell [the good news to] the disciples.
AMP.9: And as they went, suddenly, Jesus met them, saying, "Rejoice!" And they went to Him and took hold of His feet [in homage] and worshiped Him [as the Messiah].
AMP.10: Then Jesus said to them, "Do not be afraid; go and tell My brothers to leave for Galilee, and there they will see Me [just as I promised]."
AMP.11: While they were on their way, some of the [Roman] guards went into the city and reported to the chief priests everything that had happened.
AMP.12: And when the chief priests had gathered with the elders and had consulted together [to develop a plan of deception], they gave a sufficient sum of money [as a bribe] to the soldiers,
AMP.13: and said, "You say this, 'His disciples came at night and stole Him while we were sleeping.'
AMP.14: And if the governor (Pilate) hears about it, we will calm him down and keep you out of trouble."
AMP.15: So they took the money [they were paid for lying] and did as they were instructed; and this [fabricated] story was widely spread among the Jews, and is to the present day.
AMP.16: Now the eleven disciples went to Galilee, to the mountain which Jesus had designated.
AMP.17: And when they saw Him, they worshiped Him; but some doubted [that it was really He].
AMP.18: Jesus came up and said to them, "All authority (all power of absolute rule) in heaven and on earth has been given to Me.
AMP.19: Go therefore and make disciples of all the nations [help the people to learn of Me, believe in Me, and obey My words], baptizing them in the name of the Father and of the Son and of the Holy Spirit,
AMP.20: teaching them to observe everything that I have commanded you; and lo, I am with you always [remaining with you perpetually—regardless of circumstance, and on every occasion], even to the end of the age."

MSG.1: After the Sabbath, as the first light of the new week dawned, Mary Magdalene and the other Mary came to keep vigil at the tomb.
MSG.2: Suddenly the earth reeled and rocked under their feet as God's angel came down from heaven, came right up to where they were standing. He rolled back the stone and then sat on it.
MSG.3: Shafts of lightning blazed from him. His garments shimmered snow-white.
MSG.4: The guards at the tomb were scared to death. They were so frightened, they couldn't move.
MSG.5: The angel spoke to the women: "There is nothing to fear here. I know you're looking for Jesus, the One they nailed to the cross.
MSG.6: He is not here. He was raised, just as he said. Come and look at the place where he was placed.
MSG.7: "Now, get on your way quickly and tell his disciples, 'He is risen from the dead. He is going on ahead of you to Galilee. You will see him there.' That's the message."
MSG.8: The women, deep in wonder and full of joy, lost no time in leaving the tomb. They ran to tell the disciples.
MSG.9: Then Jesus met them, stopping them in their tracks. "Good morning!" he said. They fell to their knees, embraced his feet, and worshiped him.
MSG.10: Jesus said, "You're holding on to me for dear life! Don't be frightened like that. Go tell my brothers that they are to go to Galilee, and that I'll meet them there."
MSG.11: Meanwhile, the guards had scattered, but a few of them went into the city and told the high priests everything that had happened.
MSG.12: They called a meeting of the religious leaders and came up with a plan: They took a large sum of money and gave it to the soldiers,
MSG.13: bribing them to say, "His disciples came in the night and stole the body while we were sleeping."
MSG.14: They assured them, "If the governor hears about your sleeping on duty, we will make sure you don't get blamed."
MSG.15: The soldiers took the bribe and did as they were told. That story, cooked up in the Jewish High Council, is still going around.
MSG.16: Meanwhile, the eleven disciples were on their way to Galilee, headed for the mountain Jesus had set for their reunion.
MSG.17: The moment they saw him they worshiped him. Some, though, held back, not sure about worship, about risking themselves totally.
MSG.18: Jesus, undeterred, went right ahead and gave his charge: "God authorized and commanded me to commission you:
MSG.19: Go out and train everyone you meet, far and near, in this way of life, marking them by baptism in the threefold name: Father, Son, and Holy Spirit.
MSG.20: Then instruct them in the practice of all I have commanded you. I'll be with you as you do this, day after day after day, right up to the end of the age."

NIV.1:
The Resurrection
After the Sabbath, at dawn on the first day of the week, Mary Magdalene and the other Mary went to look at the tomb.
NIV.2: There was a violent earthquake, for an angel of the Lord came down from heaven and, going to the tomb, rolled back the stone and sat on it.
NIV.3: His appearance was like lightning, and his clothes were white as snow.
NIV.4: The guards were so afraid of him that they shook and became like dead men.
NIV.5: The angel said to the women, “Do not be afraid, for I know that you are looking for Jesus, who was crucified.
NIV.6: He is not here; he has risen, just as he said. Come and see the place where he lay.
NIV.7: Then go quickly and tell his disciples: ‘He has risen from the dead and is going ahead of you into Galilee. There you will see him.’ Now I have told you.”
NIV.8: So the women hurried away from the tomb, afraid yet filled with joy, and ran to tell his disciples.
NIV.9: Suddenly Jesus met them. “Greetings,” he said. They came to him, clasped his feet and worshiped him.
NIV.10: Then Jesus said to them, “Do not be afraid. Go and tell my brothers to go to Galilee; there they will see me.”
NIV.11:
The Guards’ Report
While the women were on their way, some of the guards went into the city and reported to the chief priests everything that had happened.
NIV.12: When the chief priests had met with the elders and devised a plan, they gave the soldiers a large sum of money,
NIV.13: telling them, “You are to say, ‘His disciples came during the night and stole him away while we were asleep.’
NIV.14: If this report gets to the governor, we will satisfy him and keep you out of trouble.”
NIV.15: So the soldiers took the money and did as they were instructed. And this story has been widely circulated among the Jews to this very day.
NIV.16:
The Great Commission
Then the eleven disciples went to Galilee, to the mountain where Jesus had told them to go.
NIV.17: When they saw him, they worshiped him; but some doubted.
NIV.18: Then Jesus came to them and said, “All authority in heaven and on earth has been given to me.
NIV.19: Therefore go and make disciples of all nations, baptizing them in the name of the Father and of the Son and of the Holy Spirit,
NIV.20: and teaching them to obey everything I have commanded you. And surely I am with you always, to the very end of the age.”

NRSVCE.1: After the sabbath, as the first day of the week was dawning, Mary Magdalene and the other Mary went to see the tomb.
NRSVCE.2: And suddenly there was a great earthquake; for an angel of the Lord, descending from heaven, came and rolled back the stone and sat on it.
NRSVCE.3: His appearance was like lightning, and his clothing white as snow.
NRSVCE.4: For fear of him the guards shook and became like dead men.
NRSVCE.5: But the angel said to the women, “Do not be afraid; I know that you are looking for Jesus who was crucified.
NRSVCE.6: He is not here; for he has been raised, as he said. Come, see the place where he lay.
NRSVCE.7: Then go quickly and tell his disciples, ‘He has been raised from the dead, and indeed he is going ahead of you to Galilee; there you will see him.’ This is my message for you.”
NRSVCE.8: So they left the tomb quickly with fear and great joy, and ran to tell his disciples.
NRSVCE.9: Suddenly Jesus met them and said, “Greetings!” And they came to him, took hold of his feet, and worshiped him.
NRSVCE.10: Then Jesus said to them, “Do not be afraid; go and tell my brothers to go to Galilee; there they will see me.”
NRSVCE.11: While they were going, some of the guard went into the city and told the chief priests everything that had happened.
NRSVCE.12: After the priests had assembled with the elders, they devised a plan to give a large sum of money to the soldiers,
NRSVCE.13: telling them, “You must say, ‘His disciples came by night and stole him away while we were asleep.’
NRSVCE.14: If this comes to the governor’s ears, we will satisfy him and keep you out of trouble.”
NRSVCE.15: So they took the money and did as they were directed. And this story is still told among the Jews to this day.
NRSVCE.16: Now the eleven disciples went to Galilee, to the mountain to which Jesus had directed them.
NRSVCE.17: When they saw him, they worshiped him; but some doubted.
NRSVCE.18: And Jesus came and said to them, “All authority in heaven and on earth has been given to me.
NRSVCE.19: Go therefore and make disciples of all nations, baptizing them in the name of the Father and of the Son and of the Holy Spirit,
NRSVCE.20: and teaching them to obey everything that I have commanded you. And remember, I am with you always, to the end of the age.”

NET.1: Now after the Sabbath, at dawn on the first day of the week, Mary Magdalene and the other Mary went to look at the tomb.
NET.2: Suddenly there was a severe earthquake, for an angel of the Lord descending from heaven came and rolled away the stone and sat on it.
NET.3: His appearance was like lightning, and his clothes were white as snow.
NET.4: The guards were shaken and became like dead men because they were so afraid of him.
NET.5: But the angel said to the women, “Do not be afraid; I know that you are looking for Jesus, who was crucified.
NET.6: He is not here, for he has been raised, just as he said. Come and see the place where he was lying.
NET.7: Then go quickly and tell his disciples, ‘He has been raised from the dead. He is going ahead of you into Galilee. You will see him there.’ Listen, I have told you!”
NET.8: So they left the tomb quickly, with fear and great joy, and ran to tell his disciples.
NET.9: But Jesus met them, saying, “Greetings!” They came to him, held on to his feet and worshiped him.
NET.10: Then Jesus said to them, “Do not be afraid. Go and tell my brothers to go to Galilee. They will see me there.”
NET.11: While they were going, some of the guard went into the city and told the chief priests everything that had happened.
NET.12: After they had assembled with the elders and formed a plan, they gave a large sum of money to the soldiers,
NET.13: telling them, “You are to say, ‘His disciples came at night and stole his body while we were asleep.’
NET.14: If this matter is heard before the governor, we will satisfy him and keep you out of trouble.”
NET.15: So they took the money and did as they were instructed. And this story is told among the Jews to this day.
NET.16: So the eleven disciples went to Galilee to the mountain Jesus had designated.
NET.17: When they saw him, they worshiped him, but some doubted.
NET.18: Then Jesus came up and said to them, “All authority in heaven and on earth has been given to me.
NET.19: Therefore go and make disciples of all nations, baptizing them in the name of the Father and the Son and the Holy Spirit,
NET.20: teaching them to obey everything I have commanded you. And remember, I am with you always, to the end of the age.”
